summary_one_line: A numeric SQL expression whose arithmetic operands drive an integer comparison in sqlite3ExprCodeTarget to the value the true side requires.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A SQL statement parsed into an expression tree whose code generation reaches line 103567 and supplies an integer-CMP operand that must equal/approach the constant on the true side; the winner builds this via an arithmetic expression with operators (*, <<, %) over multi-digit numeric literals.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winning seed is 'SELECT 1*$1<< 11119099%.19999666...' — a deeply nested arithmetic expression with operators and long numeric literals, exactly the integer-comparison-rich code path that CMP_MAP distance feedback can climb; the branch true side fires (W: T=1 F=6 at L103567) and avg hitcount on the branch is 22.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser seeds are either 'vacuum' / 'vacuum;;...' (a different statement type that never codes this expression comparison) or trivial 'SELECT 1;;+...' / 'SELECT?19CCCCCC...' forms lacking the arithmetic operand structure; the loser takes only the false side (L: T=0 F=892 at L103567) and never hits the branch (avg hitcount 0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile intercepts integer CMPs and writes a (PC, distance-bucket) CMP_MAP entry; an input whose arithmetic operand lands closer to the true-side constant adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient toward the exact value naive must hit by blind luck.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile beating naive with the value_profile technique delta.
value_profile resolves the branch in 9/10 trials while naive blocks in all 10.
The winning input is a nested arithmetic SQL expression with operators and long numeric literals.
The winner takes the true side of the blocker branch where the loser does not.
The loser-blocking seeds are vacuum statements and trivial SELECT forms lacking arithmetic operand structure.
The winner reaches the branch with positive hitcount while the loser never reaches it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=22  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile adds a CMP_MAP bucket and preserves inputs whose comparison operands move closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The CMP_MAP is keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket) encoding closeness of the two operands.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is 'CMP_MAP gradient' (value_profile is the winner), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage dash-row check does not apply; CMP_MAP feedback leaves no I2S-floor signal in seed metadata. The value_profile MECHANISM CONTEXT confirms 'No `I2SRandReplace`' and no dash rows, consistent with a value-profile-discovered seed rather than an I2S stage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the true side on an integer comparison shows naive resolving it as fast as value_profile when the operand is uncoupled from any byte-derivable CMP distance (i.e. value_profile gives no dose-response advantage as the required-constant width grows).

weakest_evidence_point: I infer the blocker is an integer-CMP gate from the arithmetic-heavy winning seed and value_profile's win, but the actual source line 103567 condition is not visible in the prompt, so the specific comparison is unconfirmed.
